description: tau-scan: sweep tau=1..50 through L1 merge lens, measure Frobenius distance from rank-1
Non-Markovianity test: compare P(X_{t+1}|X_t) vs P(X_{t+1}|X_t,X_{t-1}) via TV distance on macro trajectory through merge lens
Slow-mode-preserving merge: min-gap vs max-gap merge selection, both exact K^tau, compare rank-1 deviation
Null regime: P6 OFF (no budget), P3 OFF. Verify no kernel modifications occur.
P6 drive isolation: P6 ON (budget active), P3 OFF. Does budget-driven modification create slow-mixing structure?
P3 isolation: P3 ON (protocol cycling), P6 OFF (no budget). Does protocol phase biasing alone create structure?
Full dynamics: P6 ON + P3 ON. Does combined budget drive + protocol biasing produce non-rank-1 macro kernels?
Spectral-guided dynamics: P6 ON + P3 ON + spectral-guided P2 gating. Does targeting inter-cluster edges produce scale-independent structure?
Budget cap: spectral-guided dynamics with budget capped at n*ln(n). Does the attractor survive budget constraints?
Multi-state spectral clustering: k=4 partition using sign(v2,v3). Does multi-state observation produce richer macro structure?
Non-Markovianity on evolved kernel: after spectral-guided dynamics converge, test Markov property of macro process under bisection lens.
k=8 spectral partition: 3 eigenvectors, sign-based 8-way clustering. Does higher k produce richer structure?
k=4 with budget cap: recommended configuration combining multi-state partition with budget constraints.
Non-Markovianity with k=4 partition on evolved kernel: test if 3-state macro process has memory.
Cross-layer coupling: Level 1 P6 audit (frob) modulates Level 0 P2 budget cost for boundary-crossing moves. Spectral bisection of macro kernel defines Level 1 groups.

Two-level ladder analysis: run Level 0 dynamics (recommended config k=4, spectral, budget cap), extract evolved macro kernel, spectral bisection → adaptive tau → Level 2 macro kernel. Measure non-rank-1 at Level 2.
Dynamics on macro kernel: run Level 0 dynamics, extract 3-state macro kernel, run Level 1 dynamics on it. Tests whether dynamics engine produces structure at multiple levels.

parameters.note: Quick test only — Level 1 dynamics frozen by viability constraints

description: Three-level ladder with k=8: L0 dynamics (k=8 spectral-guided) → 4-5 state macro → L1 k=4 partition → 3-4 state macro → L2 bisection → 2-state macro. Non-rank-1 at all 3 levels?
Non-Markovianity at both levels of the three-level ladder. L0: micro trajectory → k=8 partition → NM test. L1: macro trajectory from L0 macro kernel → k=4 partition → NM test.
Coupling strength sweep: test coupling_strength in {0, 1, 2, 5, 10, 20} with k=4 recommended config. Does any strength regime amplify macro structure?
Mutual information between ladder levels: H(L0_partition) under micro pi, H(L1_partition) under micro pi, information preservation fraction. Uses k=4 recommended config at L0, bisection at L1.

Phase 1 Property Revisit: measure 6 emergent properties (chirality, MI, spectral lines, locality, DPI cascade, route mismatch) on Phase 2 evolved non-rank-1 macro kernels at all 3 levels of the k=8 three-level ladder.

SBRC test: signed boundary repair coupling with 3 repair-bias levels (0.0, 0.3, 0.6). All configs use coupling_signed=true. Fork of EXP-087 config (k=4, spectral-guided, budget cap, coupling_strength=5.0). Measures repair_frac, violation_frac, P2 acceptance rate, and frob.

note: total_steps=n*5000 (not n*10000 as in dynamics experiments). Null baseline; shorter run is sufficient since zero modifications are expected. partition_refresh_interval=500 is a compile-time constant in mixture.rs, not a per-experiment parameter.
total_steps=n*5000 (not n*10000). P3-only control; shorter run sufficient since zero modifications expected.

partition_refresh_interval=500 is a compile-time constant (PARTITION_REFRESH_INTERVAL in crates/dynamics/src/mixture.rs:223), not a configurable parameter. Applies uniformly to all spectral-guided experiments.

TODO: Extend scale_range to include n=256 and regenerate sweep to support CLO-085 n=256 claim (currently unsupported by artifact). The scaling decay pattern (halves per doubling) is confirmed for n=32/64/128.
TODO: Extend scale_range to include n=256 (same as EXP-077 amendment). CLO-085/086 n=256 values are not currently in the sweep artifact.

Sweep log regenerated with current observation code (spectral_clustering_lens from spectral.rs, replacing legacy spectral_bisection_lens from substrate.rs). New frob baseline: n=32: 0.067±0.028, n=64: 0.038±0.016, n=128: 0.029±0.008. Zero modifications confirmed in all 30 runs.
Sweep log regenerated with current observation code. Frob values identical to EXP-076 (same kernel, same zero modifications). Confirms P3-alone has no effect.

TODO: Regenerate sweep_exp080.log with current code for full consistency. The spectral partition change (spectral.rs vs substrate.rs eigenvector init) affects both dynamics and observation for spectral-guided experiments. Attractor (frob≈0.858) is robust but modification counts and transient max_frob differ. Also TODO: extend to n=256 (spot check confirms attractor holds at n=256, final_frob=0.857809).

TODO: Extend scale_range to n=256 to support CLO-094's n=256 spot check claim (1.575±0.149). Currently no sweep artifact at n=256 exists. The 1.575±0.149 value cited in CLO-094 is not traceable to any committed log.

Logging format change: current code reports both accept_rate (overall: (p1_acc+p2_acc)/total) and p2_accept_rate (P2-only: p2_acc/(p2_acc+p2_rej)). Committed sweep log only reports accept_rate which is actually the P2 accept rate. Numerically identical dynamics; output SUMMARY line format differs (added p2_accept_rate field). Re-runs under new format will NOT be bitwise identical at the log level. TODO: Regenerate sweep_exp087.log under current format, or declare artifact as pre-format-change. Also TODO: extend to n=256. CLO-094's 'vs 99%+' uncoupled accept rate claim is wrong — see CLO-094 v2 amendment.

TODO: The n=256 spot check (notebook lines 710-726, 5 seeds) is not backed by any committed sweep artifact. sweep_exp090.log contains only scale_range [32,64,128]. Either extend to n=256 and commit sweep, or label notebook n=256 table as informal/unversioned. Same issue as EXP-085 n=256 gap.

Committed sweep_exp092.log predates the p2_accept_rate field addition (CLO-102c). Current code prints both accept_rate and p2_accept_rate for EXP-092, but the committed log only contains accept_rate. Dynamics are numerically identical; only SUMMARY line format differs. TODO: Regenerate sweep_exp092.log under current format, or declare artifact as pre-format-change.
60 runs completed (10 seeds × 2 scales × 3 biases). CLO-103 written. HYP-123 NOT SUPPORTED.

description: Three-level ladder with SBRC: fork of EXP-090 (k=8 at L0, k=4 at L1, bisection at L2) with SBRC coupling enabled at L0. Tests whether signed coupling changes ladder structure.

Property diagnostics on SBRC-evolved kernels: uniform-weighted MI, sigma_u, max_asym, spectral eigenvalues, absorbing state counts, chirality metrics at all 3 levels. Fork of EXP-094 diagnostics on SBRC kernels.

P6→P3 mixer on three-level ladder: frob-driven protocol modulation (boring=1-clamp(frob/scale)) adjusts P3 mixture weights. Boring kernels get more P2/P1, interesting kernels get more trajectory/P5. 3 mixer strengths (1.0, 2.0, 4.0), with SBRC coupling. Fork of EXP-096.

note: 90 runs completed (10 seeds × 3 scales × 3 strengths). CLO-105 written. HYP-125 NOT SUPPORTED. Parallelized across 8 processes on 48-core machine.

description: Full diagnostic comparison across 5 conditions (uncoupled, SBRC, mixer×{1,2,4}) at n=32. Replays dynamics with all available diagnostics: frob, gap, sigma_pi/u, mi_pi/u, n_absorb, blocks, max_asym, cyc_mean/max, n_chiral, trans_ep, n_nontrivial, eigenvalues, macro kernel entries. Designed to test whether mixer changes macro topology beyond frob.
result: 11 large effects (|d|>0.8), 18 medium. Key: mixer reduces absorbing states (d=-1.08), increases gap (d=+1.19), reduces pi-MI (d=-1.24), increases chirality (d=+0.83). CLO-105 revised to partially_supported.

description: PICA single-cell sweep: for each of the 13 action cells (A1-A13, except A10 baseline), enable ONLY that cell + baseline P2<-P4, run dynamics, and report full diagnostics including commutator [P1,P2], [P1,P4], [P2,P4]. Uses PICA-based dynamics dispatch (not legacy code path). 3-level macro analysis at observation time.

Multi-level PICA dynamics: 3-level ladder with P1-P6 applied at every level (not just micro). Compares 4 PICA configurations: baseline (P2<-P4), SBRC (P2<-P4+P2<-P6), mixer (SBRC+P3<-P6), full_action (all 13 action cells). Each macro level gets its own AugmentedState with PICA dynamics. Inter-level coupling via kernel blending every 1000 micro steps. Uses LadderConfig::three_level.

Full PICA sweep of new cells A18-A25: 14 configs testing each new cell individually, row combos (P3_row_all, P5_row_all, P6_row_all), and full presets (full_action, full_all). Each config = baseline (A10+A15) + additional cells. Uses run_exp_100_single for full diagnostics.
result: 9 of 14 configs identical to baseline. Only 5 differ: P3_row_all (sigma_pi=0, frob=1.396), A24_only (p2_rate +56%), P6_row_all (=A24_only), full_action (repair_frac=0.53), full_all (p2_rate 0.221). A25 is inert. A18-A20, A21-A23, P5_row_all all identical to baseline due to dependency chains.

description: PICA cell synergy experiments: 16 targeted combos to understand why cells are inert alone but active together. Tests A13 decomposition (A13 alone vs +A18 vs +A18+A19 vs +A18+A20), packaging consumer+producer combos (A5+A21, A11+A22, A11+A21), budget combos (A24+A25, A13+A24, A13+A25), known presets (sbrc, mixer), and P1-row activation (A3+A13, full P1 row+A13).

result: 15 configs × 10 seeds at n=64. Key findings: (1) A13_only is mild (frob +0.064); (2) A13+A18 = A13_only; (3) A13+A18+A19 = phase transition (sigma_pi→0, frob 1.34, budget cap); (4) A13+A18+A20 = A13+A18+A19; (5) packaging combos work but small effect; (6) sbrc has repair_frac=0.64; (7) P1_row+A13 gives frob 0.995. The critical chain for sigma→0 is A13+A18+A19.

description: Phase transition investigation: sector boost scan (0.1-4.0), alternative cluster_rm enablers (A3/A14/A21 vs A18), cross-regime combos (reversible + SBRC/A24/packaging). 14 configs × 10 seeds at n=64.
result: (1) Sector boost scan: ALL values 0.1-4.0 produce IDENTICAL results. The transition is binary (presence of A19), not strength-dependent. (2) A14 does NOT produce sigma→0 (sigma=3.08) because it changes the partition itself. A3/A18/A21 all work as enablers. (3) SBRC breaks reversibility (sigma=1.68, repair_frac=0.62). A24/packaging preserve it but change p2_rate.

description: Scale dependence investigation: do key findings from n=64 (EXP-104/105/106) hold at n=128 and n=256? Tests 11 configs: baseline, chain (A13+A18+A19), A13_only, A24_only, A25_only, sbrc, full_action, full_all, A11+A22, chain+A24, chain+SBRC. Key questions: (1) does reversible transition persist at scale? (2) does A25 become active? (3) does packaging effect grow? (4) does SBRC still break reversibility?

full_action decomposition at scale: why does full_action maintain frob>1.0 at n=256 while all others drop to ~0.5? Tests 10 configs at n=256 with macro analysis at k=4,8,16. Decomposes full_action by removing subsets of cells (no SBRC, no P6 row, P1P2 rows only, etc.)
Individual cell characterization RERUN after 8 bug fixes (needs_cluster_rm, cache invalidation, EP terms, last_flip_step, A20 partition). 14 configs × 10 seeds at n=64.
result: Major changes: A19_only now triggers sigma=0 (9/10 seeds, frob=1.34). A20_only=A19_only (identical after Fix #6). P3_row_all sigma=0.00 (10/10). full_all sigma=1.56 (was 0.027 pre-fix). Inert: A18, A21-A23, A25, P5_row. Active alone: A19/A20 (reversible), A24 (budget rate).

description: Synergy experiments RERUN post-fix. 15 configs × 10 seeds at n=64.
result: Key changes: A13_A18=A13_only (A18 zero effect). A13_A25=A13_only (A25 inert). A13_A18_A19=A13_A18_A20 (A20 clone). A11_A22 sigma champion (8.27). P1_row_A13 best all-round (frob=1.0, sigma=6.15). A13 alone sigma=8.11 (strongest sigma booster). Reversible transition still 8/10 for A13+A18+A19.

description: Scale dependence RERUN post-fix. 13 configs × 10 seeds at n=128 and n=256. Added A19_only and P1_row_A13 to config list.

PICA rerun campaign v1: 6 stages with KEY_AUDIT_JSON rich audits. Stages: (01) core baselines EXP-104, (02) single-cell utility EXP-100, (03) producers/consumers EXP-102/103, (04) full system EXP-105, (05) scale/phase EXP-107/106, (06) deep dives.

parameters.audit_tier: rich for n<256, standard at n=256 unless SIX_BIRDS_AUDIT_RICH=1

description: PICA rerun campaign: analysis of 736 audit records complete with 37 findings. Pending: EXP-103 s8-9 n=128, EXP-106 post-review n=128 s3-9, n=256 data for all experiments. 27 runner processes still active.

Lagrange probe baseline survey: 13 standard configs x 10 seeds x {n=64, n=128}. Wave 1 data uses old binary (step_entropy, pla2_gap, lagr_geo_r2, lagr_diff_kl, lagr_diff_alpha). Current binary adds t_rel, gap_ratio, eigen_entropy, spectral_participation, slow_modes.
Lagrange probe scale dependence: full_action + baseline + A14_only x 10 seeds x {n=32, n=64, n=128, n=256}. Wave 1 data uses old binary. Current binary adds t_rel, gap_ratio, spectral_participation.

Audit tier updated: runner now always runs rich scan (multi_scale_scan) at all scales including n=256 (main.rs change 2026-02-24). The SIX_BIRDS_AUDIT_RICH env var is no longer needed but is still set by launch scripts for backward compat. Wave 1 data was generated by the pre-change binary and lacks multi_scale_scan for n=256 jobs.

Step count convention changed: total_steps reduced from n*10000 to n*2000 (5x reduction). Convergence analysis of 2074 existing runs showed: (1) final/max frob ratio 0.87-0.93 at n>=128, system converges well before endpoint; (2) interesting configs (full_action, A13_A14_A19) need only 2-20 partition flips, with ~92 mods/row sufficient; (3) full_action already produces signature behavior at 85 mods/row (n=64). obs_interval scaled proportionally from n*1000 to n*200 in runner (n*100 to n*20 in defaults). All experiment functions updated. All data generated before this change used the old n*10000 convention.

Systematic PICA ablation survey: 69 configs across 9 groups — controls (3: empty/baseline/full_action), single-cell retests (6: A14/A16/A17/A18/A20/A22), row ablations (6: P1-P6 rows, P5-consuming cells dropped where no producer), column ablations (6: inf P1-P6), leave-one-out from full_action (22), synergy pairs (8), row-pair interactions (4, P5-consuming cells dropped), diagnostic row/column removal from full_action (8 incl full_all), candidate generating sets (6: gen4_core/gen3_A13_A14_A19/gen5_core_A25/gen6_core_A12_A25/gen3_A13_A18_A19/gen2_A14_A13). Fixes: (1) P5 fallback impurity cleaned — A5/A11/A20 excluded from configs lacking P5 producers, A22 added as co-producer where needed; (2) headline tau uses dynamics-produced active_tau (A18/PICA) when available, consistent with multi_scale_scan. Primary question: what is the minimal generating set for each PICA regime (REV, high-frob, stable)?
